diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "EssentialsTab" is 335 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 5 useState calls in "EssentialsTab" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Non-component export in component file
diagnostics.message: This file exports non-components, so Fast Refresh can't safely preserve component state.
diagnostics.help: Move non-component exports out of component files so Fast Refresh can preserve component state instead of full-reloading.

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Boolean prop combinations are hard to test
diagnostics.message: Component "ActionVariantRenderer" takes 5 on/off props (showCategory, showResponsibles, showPartner…), which is hard to combine & test. Split it into smaller components or named variants.
diagnostics.help: Split boolean-heavy APIs into smaller components or named variants so combinations stay testable.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with a prop plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Derived value copied into state
diagnostics.message: Storing "items" in state when you can derive it from other values costs an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Work out the value while rendering (or with useMemo if it's expensive) instead of copying it into useState through a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#updating-state-based-on-props-or-state

diagnostics.title: Data passed to parent via effect
diagnostics.message: Handing data back to a parent from a useEffect costs your users an extra render.
diagnostics.help: Fetch the data in the parent and pass it down as a prop (or return it from the hook), instead of handing it back up through a prop callback in a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#passing-data-to-the-parent

diagnostics.title: Non-component export in component file
diagnostics.message: This file exports non-components, so Fast Refresh can't safely preserve component state.
diagnostics.help: Move non-component exports out of component files so Fast Refresh can preserve component state instead of full-reloading.

diagnostics.title: Non-component export in component file
diagnostics.message: This file exports non-components, so Fast Refresh can't safely preserve component state.
diagnostics.help: Move non-component exports out of component files so Fast Refresh can preserve component state instead of full-reloading.

diagnostics.title: Effect re-subscribes on a changing callback
diagnostics.message: Your effect re-subscribes whenever "getActionsMap" changes, even though it's only used inside `addEventListener`.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the callback with `useEffectEvent(callback)` (React 19+) and call it inside the sub-handler. An Effect Event always sees the latest props and state but isn't a dependency, so the effect won't re-subscribe every time the parent redraws. See https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffectEvent

diagnostics.title: State initialized from a mount effect
diagnostics.message: Your users see an extra render with empty "primaryColorIndex" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.title: State initialized from a mount effect
diagnostics.message: Your users see an extra render with empty "followPartnerColor" because a useEffect sets its starting value.
diagnostics.help: Pass the initial value directly to useState() instead of setting it from a mount-only useEffect. For SSR hydration, prefer useSyncExternalStore().

diagnostics.title: BaaS authority map shipped in browser artifact
diagnostics.message: A browser artifact exposes Firebase/Supabase config together with sensitive collections or authorization fields.
diagnostics.help: Client BaaS config is often public, but shipped collection names plus owner, role, tenant, or admin fields give attackers a precise authorization map. Verify rules/RLS enforce every boundary server-side.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: 14 useState calls in "ProfilePage" can each trigger a separate render.
diagnostics.help: Group related state in `useReducer` so one logical update does not fan out into separate renders.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with state plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: BaaS authority map shipped in browser artifact
diagnostics.message: A browser artifact exposes Firebase/Supabase config together with sensitive collections or authorization fields.
diagnostics.help: Client BaaS config is often public, but shipped collection names plus owner, role, tenant, or admin fields give attackers a precise authorization map. Verify rules/RLS enforce every boundary server-side.
